chief_complaint: A 75-year-old woman developed acute hypoxia, tachycardia, and tachypnea during ureteroscopic lithotripsy.
history: The patient has a history of hypertension and diabetes. She underwent ureteroscopic lithotripsy and developed acute hypoxia, tachycardia (110/min), and tachypnea (35/min) accompanied by a positive fluid balance of 2430 mL from irrigation absorption.
exam: Vital signs: tachycardia (110/min), tachypnea (35/min), hypoxia. Physical examination findings: none specified.
labs: ECG: not specified. Bloods: metabolic acidosis (pH 7.32), hypoxemia (PaO(2)/FiO(2) 132 mmHg), markedly elevated NT-proBNP (3892 pg/mL). Imaging: pulmonary CT angiography excluded thromboembolism but confirmed bilateral pulmonary edema. Other tests: bedside echocardiography revealed RV dilation, McConnell's sign, D-shaped LV, severe tricuspid regurgitation, elevated systolic pulmonary artery pressure (58 mmHg), 60/60 sign, and fused mitral E/A waves.
